title: microlaena thomsonii
intro: Microlaena thomsonii, also known as Thomson's grass or silky microlaena, is a species of grass native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving coughs, and treating respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Microlaena thomsonii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
